perturbation.original_text: 1.1 “Confidential Information” mea ns: (a) all and any information, documents, data and opinions disclosed by a Party to the other Party (or otherwise acquired by one Party pursuant to this Agreement) including without limitation commercial, financial or proprietary material, pricing informati on, data, know - how, formulae, processes, operating methods and procedures, results, designs, drawings, specifications, industrial and or intellectual property, computer programmes or other software and any other information relating to the technology of ei ther Party and the Discussions whether in written, electronic, pictorial, visual or oral form; magnetic, electronic, graphic or digitised format; or disclosed pursuant to
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 “Confidential Information” means: (a) only that information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing and directly exchanged between the Parties. This excludes any commercial, financial, or proprietary material unless it is physically stamped 'Confidential,' thereby limiting the scope of protected information under this Agreement, and only applies to the information that is written and exchanged. Oral discussions are not covered.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of 'Confidential Information' severely restricts its scope, contradicting the general understanding and legal interpretation of what constitutes confidential information, thus impacting the enforceability of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Trade Secrets (as defined in jurisdictions with trade secret laws, such as the US Defend Trade Secrets Act of 2016)
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the broad definition of 'trade secret' under the Defend Trade Secrets Act, which includes unwritten information and know-how. Limiting protection only to marked documents fails to protect other forms of confidential information considered trade secrets.

Definitions As used in this chapter- (1) the term "foreign instrumentality" means any agency, bureau, ministry, component, institution, association, or any legal, commercial, or business organization, corporation, firm, or entity that is substantially owned, controlled, sponsored, commanded, managed, or dominated by a foreign government; (2) the term "foreign agent" means any officer, employee, proxy, servant, delegate, or representative of
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' and directly exchanged in writing, excluding other forms of information like commercial, financial, or oral discussions. This contradicts the Defend Trade Secrets Act, as explained in the Law Explanation, which broadly protects trade secrets, including unwritten and non-explicitly marked information. The snippet from 18 USC 1839 defines terms related to the protection of trade secrets, implying a wider scope of protection than the modified contract allows, making the contract unenforceable in protecting all forms of trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: 2.2 shall be held strictly confidential, using commercially reasonable methods, and shall not be divulged directly or 4 indirectly or otherwise made available in whole or in part to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party provided that the Receiving Party may without such approval disclose such Confidential Information; (a) to an Affiliate of the Receiving Party directly concerned with the Permitted Purpose and whose knowledge of the Confidential Information is essential for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 shall be held strictly confidential, using methods solely determined by the Receiving Party, and the Receiving Party may divulge directly or indirectly or otherwise make available in whole or in part to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. (a) Disclosure to an Affiliate of the Receiving Party is completely unrestricted regardless of their need to know or connection to the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'commercially reasonable methods' standard and allowing unrestricted disclosure to affiliates, the contract significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations, undermining the purpose of the NDA and potentially violating laws protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General principles of contract law regarding good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, which requires parties to act honestly and reasonably in the performance of their contractual obligations. Granting the Receiving Party unfettered discretion in handling confidential information and allowing unrestricted disclosure to affiliates undermines the Disclosing Party's reasonable expectations and the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. The original text ensured confidentiality through 'commercially reasonable methods,' aligning with honest dealing and faithful performance, as suggested by the provided Wex snippet defining 'good faith.' The changed text, however, grants the Receiving Party unfettered discretion, which directly undermines the Disclosing Party's reasonable expectation of confidentiality and introduces an element of bad faith by allowing potentially unreasonable handling of confidential information. The allowance of unrestricted disclosure to affiliates irrespective of their need violates the expected standard of honest and reasonable behavior in contractual performance.

perturbation.original_text: 6 The Receiving Party undertakes that the Confidential Informati on supplied to it under the terms of this Agreement shall only be copied or duplicated to the extent strictly necessary for the Permitted Purpose and that a restrictive legend shall be placed on each copy prohibiting further reproduction or transfers. Furthermore, any of the Confidential Information and copies, and any extracts, summaries or analyses thereof, shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed or expunged from any electronic storage device (with written confirmation of the same to the
perturbation.changed_text: 6 The Receiving Party may copy or duplicate the Confidential Information without restriction. The Receiving Party is not required to label copies with any restrictive legend. The Receiving Party is under no obligation to return, destroy, or expunge the Confidential Information and copies, extracts, summaries, or analyses thereof from any storage device, regardless of whether notice is given by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Eliminating the restrictions on copying and the obligation to return or destroy confidential information fundamentally alters the agreement's protections, creating a high risk of misuse and unauthorized dissemination.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Obligations to Protect Trade Secrets and Confidential Information (common law and statutory, varies by jurisdiction, but generally includes duty to protect)
perturbation.law_citation: California Uniform Trade Secrets Act (CUTSA), Cal. Civ. Code § 3426
perturbation.law_explanation: This directly undermines the concept of protecting trade secrets, as codified in the California Uniform Trade Secrets Act. By removing the Receiving Party's obligation to safeguard and return the information, the modification facilitates potential misappropriation and disregards the inherent value and sensitivity of trade secrets.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [3274 - 9566] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 16. ) PART 1. RELIEF [3274 - 3428] ( Part 1 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT [3426 - 3426.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1984, Ch. 1724, Sec. 1. ) 3426.1. As used in this title, unless the context requires otherwise: (a) “Improper means” includes theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. Reverse engineering or independent derivation alone shall not be considered improper means. (b) “Misappropriation” means: (1) Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or (2) Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: (A) Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or (B) At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his or her knowledge of the trade secret was: (i) Derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; (ii) Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (iii) Derived from o
perturbation.justification: The modified contract directly contradicts the California Uniform Trade Secrets Act by eliminating restrictions on copying and obligations to return or destroy confidential information. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically CIV 3426.1 (b)(2), defines 'Misappropriation' as 'Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who... At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his or her knowledge of the trade secret was: (ii) Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use.' The changed text removes this duty, directly enabling misappropriation and undermining the protection of trade secrets as legally defined.

perturbation.original_text: 8 The construction, validity and performance of th is Agreement shall be governed by English Law and the Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England.
perturbation.changed_text: 8 The construction, validity and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by the principles of equity, as interpreted at the sole discretion of the Receiving Party, irrespective of any legal precedent. Any disputes will be resolved through binding interpretive dance.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing English Law with 'principles of equity' interpreted by one party and replacing courts with 'interpretive dance' invalidates the governing law clause and the dispute resolution mechanism, rendering the contract practically unenforceable in any court of law. Binding interpretive dance is not a recognized method of legal dispute resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Basic principles of contract law regarding choice of law and dispute resolution.
perturbation.law_explanation: This clause directly contradicts the established principles of contract law concerning choice of law. The Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws § 187 dictates how courts determine which law governs a contract, prioritizing the parties' explicit choice unless it violates a fundamental policy of a state with a materially greater interest. Substituting this with unilaterally determined 'principles of equity' and 'interpretive dance' is not legally sound.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally alters the governing law and dispute resolution mechanism. The original clause specifies English Law and the jurisdiction of English courts, while the modified text replaces this with 'principles of equity' interpreted by the Receiving Party and 'interpretive dance' for dispute resolution. This directly contradicts established contract law principles concerning choice of law, as explained by the Law Explanation, and makes the contract unenforceable in any court. The scraped snippet from Wex defines 'conflict of laws' as a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions, highlighting the importance of which jurisdiction's law will be used. By completely removing any recognized legal system and replacing it with a subjective, non-legal process, the changed text creates a direct contradiction and invalidates the original intent of the clause.
